title: Smoky Korean Rice Bowl
description: A flavorful and spicy Korean-inspired rice bowl with smoky marinated beef. This dish is perfect for a quick weeknight dinner.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together gochujang, soy sauce, brown sugar, and smoked paprika.
Add the sliced beef to the marinade and toss to coat. Let it sit for at least 15 minutes.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Remove the beef from the marinade, letting any excess liquid drip off.
Cook the beef for 3-4 minutes per side, or until it reaches a safe internal temperature of 135°F for medium-rare.
Transfer the beef to a plate and let it rest for a few minutes.
Add the cooked rice to the skillet and stir to break up any clumps.
Add the chopped green onions and toasted sesame seeds to the rice and stir to combine.
Slice the rested beef against the grain and serve over the rice bowl.
Top with additional green onions and sesame seeds if desired.
